title: ప్రహ్లాదుడి అద్భుత భక్తి కథ
moral: నువ్వు మంచిని మనస్ఫూర్తిగా నమ్మితే, నీకు ఎప్పుడూ ఒంటరితనం ఉండదు.
main_character: Prahlada is a small, five-year-old prince with large, luminous eyes and a gentle smile that rarely leaves his face. He often hums a soft, melodious tune to himself while walking through the palace gardens. Always the same character across every scene.
setting: The story is set in the opulent golden palace of King Hiranyakashipu, a vast structure with towering marble pillars, intricate carvings, and sprawling gardens filled with fragrant jasmine and marigold flowers. The central throne room is immense, designed to inspire awe and fear. Same location across every scene.

scenes.text: ఒకనాడు, అందమైన ప్రహ్లాదుడు పాఠశాలలో ఉన్నాడు. అతని చిన్న వేళ్ళు — చల్లటి పలక మీద మెల్లగా కదిలాయి. అందరూ హిరణ్యకశిపుడి గొప్పతనం నేర్చుకుంటున్నారు. కానీ ప్రహ్లాదుడు కళ్ళు మూసుకున్నాడు — పెదవులపై చిన్న నవ్వు. మెల్లగా "నారాయణా, నారాయణా" అని పాడుకుంటున్నాడు. గురువుగారు కోపంగా చూశారు, "ప్రహ్లాదా! నేను చెప్పేది విను! మీ నాన్నగారే సర్వశక్తిమంతుడు!" అని గద్దించారు. మరి రాజుగారికి తెలిస్తే ఏం జరుగుతుందో కదా?
scenes.image_prompt: Prince Prahlada sits in his royal classroom, softly chanting 'Narayana' instead of the lesson about his father's power. Character feels Calmly focused — eyes closed, a small smile on his lips.. The smooth, cool feel of the polished slate under his small fingers.. Prahlada is a small, five-year-old prince with large, luminous eyes and a gentle smile that rarely leaves his face. He often hums a soft, melodious tune to himself while walking through the palace gardens. Always the same character across every scene. The story is set in the opulent golden palace of King Hiranyakashipu, a vast structure with towering marble pillars, intricate carvings, and sprawling gardens filled with fragrant jasmine and marigold flowers. The central throne room is immense, designed to inspire awe and fear. Same location across every scene. Soft golden afternoon sunlight, warm hopeful mood. Hand-painted children's storybook illustration, classic Indian Chandamama and Amar Chitra Katha style, soft watercolor textures, rounded friendly shapes, warm earth-tone palette (terracotta, cream, sage green, warm ochre), clean composition with breathing space. NOT photorealistic. NOT 3D render. NOT anime. NOT dark or gritty. NO cracked earth, NO apocalyptic atmosphere, NO aggressive poses, NO spread wings unless scene is explicitly about flying, NO menacing expressions. Characters should look gentle, friendly, and age-appropriate for children 5-10.
scenes.text_te_en: Okanaadu, Andamaina Prahlaadudu Paathashaalalo Unnaadu. Atani Chinna Vellu — Challati Palaka Mida Mellagaa Kadilaayi. Andaru Hiranyakashipudi Goppatanam Nerchukuntunnaaru. Kaani Prahlaadudu Kallu Musukunnaadu — Pedavulapai Chinna Navvu. Mellagaa "Naaraayanaa, Naaraayanaa" Ani Paadukuntunnaadu. Guruvugaaru Kopangaa Chushaaru, "Prahlaadaa! Nenu Cheppedi Vinu! Mi Naannagaare Sarvashaktimantudu!" Ani Gaddinchaaru. Mari Raajugaariki Teliste Em Jarugutundo Kadaa?
scenes.text_en: One day, lovely Prahlad was at school. His tiny fingers moved gently on the cool slate. Everyone was learning about Hiranyakashipu's greatness. But Prahlad closed his eyes — a little smile on his lips. He was softly chanting, "Narayana, Narayana." The teacher looked angrily, "Prahlad! Listen to what I'm saying! Your father is the most powerful of all!" he scolded. What would happen if the king found out, right?

scenes.text: గురువుగారు చెప్పగానే, హిరణ్యకశిపుడు ప్రహ్లాదుడిని పిలిపించాడు. ఆ పెద్ద బంగారు దర్బారులో — రాజుగారి గంభీర స్వరం ప్రతిధ్వనించింది. "నీకు గొప్పవాడెవరు? నేనా, విష్ణువా?" అని కోపంగా అడిగాడు. ప్రహ్లాదుడు భయం లేకుండా నిలబడ్డాడు — తండ్రి కళ్ళలోకి చూశాడు. "తండ్రీ! నారాయణుడే సర్వాంతర్యామి, సర్వశక్తిమంతుడు" అన్నాడు. రాజుగారు విపరీతంగా కోపగించుకున్నారు. మరి తన కొడుకుకి ఎలాంటి శిక్ష వేస్తారో... ఊహించగలరా?
scenes.image_prompt: King Hiranyakashipu summons Prahlada to the throne room and angrily demands to know who is greater, him or Vishnu. Character feels Fearless and resolute — standing straight, looking his father in the eye.. The booming echo of the king's voice bouncing off the high, golden ceiling.. Prahlada is a small, five-year-old prince with large, luminous eyes and a gentle smile that rarely leaves his face. He often hums a soft, melodious tune to himself while walking through the palace gardens. Always the same character across every scene. The story is set in the opulent golden palace of King Hiranyakashipu, a vast structure with towering marble pillars, intricate carvings, and sprawling gardens filled with fragrant jasmine and marigold flowers. The central throne room is immense, designed to inspire awe and fear. Same location across every scene. Diffused daylight, gentle curious mood (NOT dark, NOT stormy). Hand-painted children's storybook illustration, classic Indian Chandamama and Amar Chitra Katha style, soft watercolor textures, rounded friendly shapes, warm earth-tone palette (terracotta, cream, sage green, warm ochre), clean composition with breathing space. NOT photorealistic. NOT 3D render. NOT anime. NOT dark or gritty. NO cracked earth, NO apocalyptic atmosphere, NO aggressive poses, NO spread wings unless scene is explicitly about flying, NO menacing expressions. Characters should look gentle, friendly, and age-appropriate for children 5-10.
scenes.text_te_en: Guruvugaaru Cheppagaane, Hiranyakashipudu Prahlaadudini Pilipinchaadu. Aa Pedda Bangaaru Darbaarulo — Raajugaari Gambhira Svaram Pratidhvaninchindi. "Niku Goppavaadevaru? Nenaa, Vishnuvaa?" Ani Kopangaa Adigaadu. Prahlaadudu Bhayam Lekundaa Nilabaddaadu — Tandri Kallaloki Chushaadu. "Tandri! Naaraayanude Sarvaantaryaami, Sarvashaktimantudu" Annaadu. Raajugaaru Viparitangaa Kopaginchukunnaaru. Mari Tana Kodukuki Elaanti Shiksha Vestaaro... Uhinchagalaraa?
scenes.text_en: As soon as the teacher told him, Hiranyakashipu called Prahlad. In that grand golden court — the king's stern voice echoed. "Who is greater to you? Me, or Vishnu?" he asked angrily. Prahlad stood without fear — looking into his father's eyes. "Father! Narayana is omnipresent and all-powerful," he said. The king became extremely angry. What kind of punishment would he give his son... can you imagine?

scenes.text: ప్రహ్లాదుడి మాటలకు రాజుగారు మండిపడ్డారు. "ఈ కొండ మీద నుండి పడేయండి!" అని ఆజ్ఞాపించారు. ప్రహ్లాదుడు "నారాయణా" అంటూ కళ్ళు మూసుకున్నాడు. ఎంత విచిత్రమో చూడండి — అతను కింద పడలేదు. మెల్లగా, తేలికగా — పూలపాన్పు మీద వాలినట్లు అనిపించింది. గాలిలో వేలాది మల్లెల సువాసన నిండింది. కింద ఉన్న సైనికులు ఆశ్చర్యపోయారు, "రాజకుమారుడికి ఏమీ కాలేదు! ఇది అద్భుతం!" అని కేకలు వేశారు. రాజుగారి ప్రణాళిక విఫలమైంది. మరింకేం చేస్తారో?
scenes.image_prompt: At the king's command, Prahlada is taken to the highest cliff, but as he falls, he feels as if he is gently floating down onto a bed of soft flowers. Character feels Trusting and serene — chanting Narayana's name even as he is carried away.. The scent of a thousand jasmine flowers blooming in the air around him.. Prahlada is a small, five-year-old prince with large, luminous eyes and a gentle smile that rarely leaves his face. He often hums a soft, melodious tune to himself while walking through the palace gardens. Always the same character across every scene. scenes.text_te_en: Prahlaadudi Maatalaku Raajugaaru Mandipaddaaru. "I Konda Mida Nundi Padeyandi!" Ani Aajnyaapinchaaru. Prahlaadudu "Naaraayanaa" Antu Kallu Musukunnaadu. Enta Vichitramo Chudandi — Atanu Kinda Padaledu. Mellagaa, Telikagaa — Pulapaanpu Mida Vaalinatlu Anipinchindi. Gaalilo Velaadi Mallela Suvaasana Nindindi. Kinda Unna Sainikulu Aashcharyapoyaaru, "Raajakumaarudiki Emi Kaaledu! Idi Adbhutan!" Ani Kekalu Veshaaru. Raajugaari Pranaalika Viphalamaindi. Marinkem Chestaaro?
scenes.text_en: The king was furious at Prahlad's words. "Throw him from this mountain!" he commanded. Prahlad closed his eyes, chanting "Narayana." See how strange it was — he didn't fall down. Gently, lightly — it felt like he landed on a bed of flowers. The air was filled with the scent of thousands of jasmine flowers. The soldiers below were astonished, "The prince is unharmed! It's a miracle!" they shouted. The king's plan failed. What would he do next?

scenes.text: రాజకుమారుడు ప్రాణాలతో ఉండటం చూసి, హిరణ్యకశిపుడు ఇంకా కోపంగా మారాడు. "హోళికా, ప్రహ్లాదుడిని ఒడిలో పెట్టుకుని అగ్నిలో కూర్చో!" అన్నాడు. హోళిక అగ్నిగుండం మీద కూర్చుంది — ప్రహ్లాదుడు ఆమె ఒడిలో. ప్రహ్లాదుడు కళ్ళు మూసుకున్నాడు — "ఓం నమో నారాయణాయ" అని జపిస్తున్నాడు. చుట్టూ దివ్యమైన కాంతి — ఒక రక్షణ కవచంలా వెలిగింది. మంటలు పెరిగాయి, కానీ ప్రహ్లాదుడికి చల్లని గాలి తాకినట్లు అనిపించింది. అగ్ని దైవశక్తి ముందు నిలవలేకపోయింది. మరి రాజుగారు ఏం చేస్తారు?
scenes.image_prompt: Holika sits on a pyre with Prahlada in her lap, but as the flames rise, Prahlada feels a cool, gentle breeze while he meditates on Lord Vishnu. Character feels Deeply meditative — unaware of the danger, lost in his devotion.. The sight of glowing divine light forming a protective shield around Prahlada.. scenes.text_te_en: Raajakumaarudu Praanaalato Undatam Chusi, Hiranyakashipudu Inkaa Kopangaa Maaraadu. "Holikaa, Prahlaadudini Odilo Pettukuni Agnilo Kurcho!" Annaadu. Holika Agnigundam Mida Kurchundi — Prahlaadudu Aame Odilo. Prahlaadudu Kallu Musukunnaadu — "Om Namo Naaraayanaaya" Ani Japistunnaadu. Chuttu Divyamaina Kaanti — Oka Rakshana Kavachanlaa Veligindi. Mantalu Perigaayi, Kaani Prahlaadudiki Challani Gaali Taakinatlu Anipinchindi. Agni Daivashakti Mundu Nilavalekapoyindi. Mari Raajugaaru Em Chestaaru?
scenes.text_en: Seeing the prince alive, Hiranyakashipu became even angrier. "Holika, take Prahlad on your lap and sit in the fire!" he ordered. Holika sat on the bonfire — Prahlad in her lap. Prahlad closed his eyes — chanting "Om Namo Narayanaya." Around him, a divine light — shone like a protective shield. The flames grew, but Prahlad felt as if a cool breeze touched him. The fire could not stand before divine power. What would the king do now?

scenes.text: అగ్ని నుండి కూడా ప్రహ్లాదుడు సురక్షితంగా బయటపడ్డాడు. రాజుగారి కోపం హద్దులు దాటింది — ఆయన సింహాసనం నుండి లేచాడు. ఒక పెద్ద స్తంభం వైపు వేలు చూపాడు. "ఈ స్తంభంలో ఉన్నాడా నీ నారాయణుడు?" అని గద్దించాడు. ప్రహ్లాదుడు ధైర్యంగా తల ఊపాడు — "అవును, తండ్రీ!" అన్నాడు. హిరణ్యకశిపుడు తన గదతో ఆ స్తంభాన్ని బలంగా కొట్టాడు. ఒక పదునైన చప్పుడుతో — పాలరాతి స్తంభం బద్దలైంది. కానీ ఇప్పుడు ఆ స్తంభం లోపల నుండి ఏం బయటకు వస్తుందో...
scenes.image_prompt: Enraged, Hiranyakashipu points to a pillar in the court and screams, 'Is your Vishnu in this pillar?' before striking it with his mace. Character feels Confident and unwavering — he nods with absolute certainty.. The sharp, cracking sound of the marble pillar splitting apart.. Prahlada is a small, five-year-old prince with large, luminous eyes and a gentle smile that rarely leaves his face. He often hums a soft, melodious tune to himself while walking through the palace gardens. Always the same character across every scene. NOT photorealistic. NOT 3D render. NOT anime. NOT dark or gritty. Characters should look gentle, friendly, and age-appropriate for children 5-10.
scenes.text_te_en: Agni Nundi Kudaa Prahlaadudu Surakshitangaa Bayatapaddaadu. Raajugaari Kopam Haddulu Daatindi — Aayana Sinhaasanam Nundi Lechaadu. Oka Pedda Stambham Vaipu Velu Chupaadu. "I Stambhanlo Unnaadaa Ni Naaraayanudu?" Ani Gaddinchaadu. Prahlaadudu Dhairyangaa Tala Upaadu — "Avunu, Tandri!" Annaadu. Hiranyakashipudu Tana Gadato Aa Stambhaanni Balangaa Kottaadu. Oka Padunaina Chappuduto — Paalaraati Stambham Baddalaindi. Kaani Ippudu Aa Stambham Lopala Nundi Em Bayataku Vastundo...
scenes.text_en: Prahlad came out safely even from the fire. The king's anger knew no bounds — he rose from his throne. He pointed his finger towards a large pillar. "Is your Narayana in this pillar?" he roared. Prahlad bravely nodded — "Yes, Father!" he said. Hiranyakashipu struck the pillar forcefully with his mace. With a sharp crack — the marble pillar shattered. But now, what would come out from inside that pillar...?

scenes.text: స్తంభం బద్దలైన క్షణంలో — భూమి కంపించింది. ఒక గొప్ప సింహనాదం — ప్రపంచాన్నే నిశ్శబ్దం చేసింది. అద్భుతమైన నరసింహ రూపం — ఆ స్తంభం నుండి బయటపడింది. ప్రహ్లాదుడు ఆశ్చర్యంతో మోకాళ్ళపై పడ్డాడు — చేతులు జోడించాడు. కళ్ళల్లో ఆనంద భాష్పాలు — భక్తితో నిండిపోయాడు. ఒక్కసారిగా దివ్యమైన కాంతి వెలిగింది — న్యాయం జరిగింది. చెడు శక్తి భగవంతుని ముందు నిలువలేకపోయింది. చిన్న ప్రహ్లాదుడు తన నారాయణుడిని చూశాడు — అది కదా నిజమైన విజయం!
scenes.image_prompt: A magnificent form of Lord Narasimha emerges from the pillar with a world-shaking roar, and in a flash of brilliant light, justice is done. Character feels Awestruck and devoted — falls to his knees with folded hands, tears of joy in his eyes.. The deep, ground-shaking roar that silences every other sound in the universe.. Prahlada is a small, five-year-old prince with large, luminous eyes and a gentle smile that rarely leaves his face. He often hums a soft, melodious tune to himself while walking through the palace gardens. Always the same character across every scene. The story is set in the opulent golden palace of King Hiranyakashipu, a vast structure with towering marble pillars, intricate carvings, and sprawling gardens filled with fragrant jasmine and marigold flowers. The central throne room is immense, designed to inspire awe and fear. Same location across every scene. Warm golden-hour glow, triumphant peaceful mood. Hand-painted children's storybook illustration, classic Indian Chandamama and Amar Chitra Katha style, soft watercolor textures, rounded friendly shapes, warm earth-tone palette (terracotta, cream, sage green, warm ochre), clean composition with breathing space. NOT photorealistic. NOT 3D render. NOT anime. NOT dark or gritty. NO cracked earth, NO apocalyptic atmosphere, NO aggressive poses, NO spread wings unless scene is explicitly about flying, NO menacing expressions. Characters should look gentle, friendly, and age-appropriate for children 5-10.
scenes.text_te_en: Stambham Baddalaina Kshananlo — Bhumi Kampinchindi. Oka Goppa Sinhanaadam — Prapanchaanne Nishshabdam Chesindi. Adbhutamaina Narasinha Rupam — Aa Stambham Nundi Bayatapadindi. Prahlaadudu Aashcharyanto Mokaallapai Paddaadu — Chetulu Jodinchaadu. Kallallo Aananda Bhaashpaalu — Bhaktito Nindipoyaadu. Okkasaarigaa Divyamaina Kaanti Veligindi — Nyaayam Jarigindi. Chedu Shakti Bhagavantuni Mundu Niluvalekapoyindi. Chinna Prahlaadudu Tana Naaraayanudini Chushaadu — Adi Kadaa Nijamaina Vijayan!
scenes.text_en: The moment the pillar shattered — the earth trembled. A mighty roar — silenced the whole world. The amazing Narasimha form — emerged from that pillar. Prahlad fell to his knees in wonder — joining his hands. Tears of joy in his eyes — he was filled with devotion. Suddenly, a divine light shone — justice was served. Evil power could not stand before God. Little Prahlad saw his Narayana — that was true victory!

quality_scores.notes: The story's greatest strengths are its flawless modern Telugu and its masterful use of oral storytelling techniques, particularly the direct questions that create irresistible suspense for a child. The narrative arc is classic and well-paced. The single most important fix is to eliminate the direct reporting of emotions; instead of stating 'రాజుగారికి కోపం వచ్చింది', show his fury through action—a trembling hand, a voice cracking with rage, or a sharp gesture—to achieve true emotional depth and elevate the story to an award-winning level.

moral_te_en: Nuvvu Manchini Manasphurtigaa Nammite, Niku Eppudu Ontaritanam Undadu.
moral_en: If you truly believe in good with all your heart, you will never be alone.
